title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
summary: Jack Roberts describes OpenAI's Codex /goal command, which lets developers describe end states rather than babysitting step-by-step code generation. The tool enters an autonomous loop called Ralph that plans, executes, tests, and self-corrects to produce working code from high-level specifications.
writeup: The video covers OpenAI's Codex /goal command, which lets developers specify a desired end state instead of directing code generation step by step. It explains the autonomous loop, called Ralph, that plans, executes, tests, and self-corrects to turn high-level specifications into working code. The framing matters because it shifts the developer's role from supervising each output to defining goals, drawing on agentic loops and test-time compute. The key takeaway: a recursive, self-correcting loop produces working code from a high-level description.
